name: Brunch Spud Boats
recipeIngredient: 3 ounces Chopped lean ham or Canadian bacon
recipeInstructions:
To make potato shells: Prick potatoes with a fork and bake in a microwave oven on a plate on high (100 percent) 7 to 11 minutes, rotating after 3 minutes, until tender when pierced with a knife. Or bake in a conventional oven at 450 degrees Fahrenheit for 50 to 60 minutes. They should be soft but firm enough to dice.
Preheat the oven's broil setting. Cut potatoes in half lengthwise and scoop out pulp, leaving a 1/2-inch shell. Dice the scooped-out potato. Spray both sides of the potato shell with cooking spray. Place the right side up on a baking sheet and broil until edges are browned and top is slightly crisp but not dry, 3 to 4 minutes. (Shells may be refrigerated overnight. Refrigerate cooked potato separately. Bring both to room temperature before filling.)
To make filling: Preheat the oven to 500 degrees Fahrenheit. In a large skillet, preferably non-stick, over high heat, heat 1 tablespoon of oil until hot. Saute the potato pulp, turning with a spatula until they begin to turn golden, about 5 minutes, adding more oil if needed. Stir in ham, zucchini, and red pepper and saute, stirring, until vegetables soften slightly, about 3 minutes. Add eggs, reduce heat to medium-low, and cook, stirring lightly, until very loosely set. Stir in green onions. Fill potato shells and sprinkle tops with cheese, if desired. Place potatoes on the baking sheet and bake for 5 minutes, or until eggs are set and cheese melts. Serve with salsa, if desired.
